title: Cannot Move Items to a Managed Board
excerpt:
I am trying to move items from a board onto a new board, which is an instance of a managed template. I am receiving an error: Sorry for the inconvenience, we couldn’t move 1 [Item] 
What am I doing wrong? All the columns…

posts.author: klewis (Karli Lewis)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am trying to move items from a board onto a new board, which is an instance of a managed template. I am receiving an error: Sorry for the inconvenience, we couldn’t move 1 [Item]
What am I doing wrong? All the columns match up and everything.

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @klewis

Just a quick suggestion before running the automation to move an item to the newly created board:

  1. Check board permissions – Ensure that both the source and destination boards have the same permissions set.
  2. Test with basic fields – If the issue persists, try running the automation using only basic fields (e.g., text or status).
    Sometimes, file columns or subtask fields can cause unexpected behavior.
  3. Review subtask structure – Double-check the columns and their values at the subtask level, as inconsistencies there can often lead to automation errors.

posts.author: Erica407Den (Erica407Den)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello!
The managed template might be restricting item movement, even if columns match. Some column types like formula or dependency may block transfers, while permissions or board settings could also be a factor. Try using a cross-board automation, updating settings, or checking for restrictions.

posts.author: KirstyM (Kirsty)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I’ve also found that if the drop downs/statuses don’t match there can be issued with templates and moving things around - so us a column template
